titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Detroit Free Press Shirley Chisholm photographs 2026.006
titlestmt.author: Dee Bowers

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-05-21 21:00:41 +0000.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

did.unittitle: Detroit Free Press Shirley Chisholm photographs
did.abstract: Four 8x10" black and white photographs of Shirley Chisholm taken on June 23, 1969 by Jimmy Tafoya, staff photographer at the Detroit Free Press.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This small collection is housed in a single folder with no particular arrangement.

bioghist.head: Biographical note
bioghist.p: Shirley Anita Chisholm (nee St. Hill) was born in New York City on November 10, 1924. When she was two years old, her family relocated from Brooklyn to her mother's native Barbados, where they remained until Chisholm was 10. She attended Brooklyn College and Columbia University. In 1964 she was elected to the New York State Assembly as a Democrat representing the Brooklyn neighborhoods of Bushwick and Bedford-Stuyvesant. In 1968, she was elected to the United States House of Representatives, the first Black woman to have done so. She served for seven terms. She also sought the Democratic presidential nomination in 1972. After retiring from Congress in 1982, Chisholm was appointed to the Purington Chair at Mount Holyoke College and led the National Political Congress of Black women.

list.head: Source
list.item: Wilson, Sule Greg C. "Chisholm, Shirley." In The Encyclopedia of New York City, edited by Kenneth T. Jackson, p245. New Haven, Conn.: Yale University Press; New York: New-York Historical Society, 2010.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: If digital surrogates exist, they should be used in place of the originals whenever possible.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Identification of item, date (if known); Detroit Free Press Shirley Chisholm photographs, 2026.026, Box and Folder number; Brooklyn Public Library, Center for Brooklyn History.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Processed by Dee Bowers, 2026. The photographs were individually sleeved in mylar and housed together in an acid-free folder.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Chisholm, Shirley. Unbought and unbossed. Boston : Houghton Mifflin, 1970. Call number CT275.C45 A3 1970
Chisholm, Shirley. The Good Fight. Harper & Row, [1973]. Call number 320.973 C
Brownmiller, Susan. Shirley Chisholm ; a biography. Garden City, N.Y. : Doubleday, [1970]. Call number CT275.C45 B76 1970
Hicks, Nancy. The Honorable Shirley Chisholm, Congresswoman from Brooklyn. New York : Lion Books, 1971. Call number B CHISHOLM H
Duffy, Susan. Shirley Chisholm : a bibliography of writings by and about her. Metuchen, N.J. : Scarecrow Press, [1988]. Call number 016.328 CHISHOLM D
Chisholm '72 unbought & unbossed. Beverly Hills, Calif. : 20th Century Fox Home Entertainment, [2004]. Call number DVD 324.973 C
Democratic Party. Congresswoman Chisholm reports to constituents. [New York : The Party?, 1970]. Call number CT275.C45 D46p 1970
Sokol, Jason. All eyes are upon us : race and politics from Boston to Brooklyn. New York : Basic Books, 2014. Call number 323.1196 S
Jeffrey Gerson Brooklyn politics research collection 2013.021
Khalil Abdulkhabir photographs of the Dar-ul-Islam movement 2020.002
Martin L. Severe collection of first day covers 2013.014

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four 8x10" black and white photographs of Shirley Chisholm taken on June 23, 1969 by Jimmy Tafoya, staff photographer at the Detroit Free Press. All four photographs are stamped and dated on the verso. The markings are consistent with those of a newspaper photograph morgue. One photo has an August 10, 1971 clipping attached to the verso regarding the public perception of Chisholm's possible run for president.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: While many items at the Center for Brooklyn History are unrestricted, we do not own reproduction rights to all materials. Be aware of the several kinds of rights that might apply: copyright, licensing and trademarks. The researcher assumes all responsibility for copyright questions.
